item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed the picture to be CC-BY-SA 3.0 or w/e compliant.

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I liked the creepiness of the second half, I hope it translates into the reading:

https://youtu.be/OZAK7I5kMiI

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Sure, that’s good.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

I am hesitant to propose a crosslink to an Short Works contest entry, due to the nature of said works, and think such crosslinks should be limited to inline links. kinch mentioned above that the infected look like those infected by SCP-1390, which provides a good potential link between the two skips. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

Instances of SCP-2690-A are [DATA EXPUNGED].

To this:

Instances of SCP-2690-A are [DATA EXPUNGED].

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright! I will read that later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They look like whatever someone infected w/ SCP-1390 eventually looks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to know what a "transformed" subject would look like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a dead thing that loves attention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is something that appears to be the corpse of an angel. People who look at it turn into similar creatures, and for some reason the Foundation thinks that if they don't feed it, it will turn everyone into an angel. So they isolate it and the people it has converted; they wrote the entry in the collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand this one. Can someone please explain it to me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know about Guiliman but I find the numerals to be stylistically jarring, both APA and MLA would have five and two written as words, and MLA would write out ten as well. I don't think there is a commonly accepted style that would use numerals for two and five.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad, but it just didn't really grab me. I suppose I've seen too many articles at this point about Abrahamic horrors that will really disturb the hell out of reality if we don't appease them. This one didn't really step far enough outside of that well-worn path for me to want to see it preserved on the mainlist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be honest, even if I wrote this for… Not the contest, I would have expunged what the 2690-A are until the end. I am a big fan of expungements that you can later fill in with info from te article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. Ambitious. And well done. +1 (after considering downvoting because, hey, [data expunged] = [oops, word count reached!]

I'm upvoting because I like the idea of the cultists actually preventing the abomination from getting loose rather than vice versa.

Yeah, I agree with Malum Lupus here!

And counter SERAPHIM-2690 with ICARUS-2690… <giggle>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't really like it that much, sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the 10 5 2. It makes me think that the effect is leaking out into the foundation as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still think you need to define what 'studying' entails (and maybe add in some of the stuff from your reply to MrWrong!), but I still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As weizhong said, the adoration effect is something I've seen before and gotten quite tired of, but I'm upvoting because I like the idea of the cultists actually preventing the abomination from getting loose rather than vice versa. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This summed it up for me, but I am reeeeeally tired of the "devoted adoration" effect, especially when it's written like this. I downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm normally weary of articles in-universe being written by those compromised by the SCP in question. I also feel that the expungement, while placed well, is there only because we have a word limit imposed and there's a bonus for hitting under 237 words. Despite this, the level this was executed at is convincing enough for me to neutral-vote, though it is something I need to think on.

I get what you're going for, as well as the implications, but some of the liberties I want to take in order to believe this can't be done for me. For me, this would have worked better and hit harder if expanded farther.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels very ophite, specifically very SCP-1348, and that's never a bad thing. I do think that the second part of the Description could be toned down just a little, but overall this is very, very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While a part of me is slightly sad that I am not getting more of this mythos (yeah, off to read the rest of your stuff) and the exact nature of its effects when not being studied, this was incredibly grand despite the article itself being so condensed. It's got the punch that the best religious anomalies around here have, wrapped up in this small cult of something… something truly eldritch.

Just, bravo.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the writing of the people who've been transformed. They're not angels, but they certainly believe they are. They're disgusting things, in my head. And they need his Light to keep living, and they want to share it. so they can fly with their useless wings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pros:

  • The first half is interesting and serves as a decent hook, although we do have similar SCPs that operate as such (people who interact with objects become affected by it).

Cons:

  • The second description comes off as melodramatic to me. I'm not sure what to make of it.

Verdict: Neutral vote (provisionally)

Question: How does the second description relate to the LK class scenario?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is, pretty obviously, for the short form contest. I'm PRETTY sure this baby clocks in at 236 words exactly. I've been playing with this idea for a very, very, very long time, but I thought now was the time to drop it. It does have some Easter Eggs, regarding some of my other scps, too. Well, just one, really.
